segments.text: The burden of the word of the LORD to Israel by Malachi

footnotes: by…: Heb. by the hand of

segments.text: I have loved you
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Wherein hast thou loved us?
segments.speaker: Israel

segments.text: Was not Esau Jacob's brother?
Yet I loved Jacob
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I hated Esau, and laid his mountains and his heritage waste for the dragons of the wilderness
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: We are impoverished, but we will return and build the desolate places
segments.speaker: Edom

segments.text: They shall build, but I will throw down; and they shall call them, The border of wickedness, and, The people against whom the LORD hath indignation for ever
segments.speaker: The LORD of hosts

segments.text: And your eyes shall see, and ye shall say
The LORD will be magnified from the border of Israel
segments.speaker: Israel

footnotes: from: or, upon: Heb. from upon

segments.text: A son honoureth his father, and a servant his master
If then I be a father, where is mine honour? and if I be a master, where is my fear? unto you, O priests, that despise my name
segments.speaker: The LORD of hosts

segments.text: Wherein have we despised thy name?
segments.speaker: Priests

segments.text: Ye offer polluted bread upon mine altar
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Wherein have we polluted thee?
segments.speaker: Priests

segments.text: The table of the LORD is contemptible
segments.speaker: Priests

segments.text: If ye offer the blind for sacrifice, is it not evil? and if ye offer the lame and sick, is it not evil? offer it now unto thy governor; will he be pleased with thee, or accept thy person?
segments.speaker: The LORD of hosts

segments.text: I pray you, beseech God that he will be gracious unto us: this hath been by your means: will he regard your persons?
segments.speaker: The LORD of hosts

footnotes: God: Heb. the face of God

segments.text: Who is there even among you that would shut the doors for nought? neither do ye kindle fire on mine altar for nought. I have no pleasure in you, neither will I accept an offering at your hand
segments.speaker: The LORD of hosts

segments.text: From the rising of the sun even unto the going down of the same my name shall be great among the Gentiles; and in every place incense shall be offered unto my name, and a pure offering: for my name shall be great among the heathen
segments.speaker: The LORD of hosts

segments.text: But ye have profaned it, in that ye say, The table of the LORD is polluted; and the fruit thereof, even his meat, is contemptible
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Behold, what a weariness is it!
segments.speaker: Priests

segments.text: Ye have snuffed at it; and ye brought that which was torn, and the lame, and the sick; thus ye brought an offering: should I accept this of your hand?
segments.speaker: The LORD of hosts

footnotes: and ye have…: or, whereas ye might have blown it away

segments.text: Cursed be the deceiver, which hath in his flock a male, and voweth, and sacrificeth unto the Lord a corrupt thing: for I am a great King, and my name is dreadful among the heathen
segments.speaker: The LORD of hosts

footnotes: which…: Heb. in whose flock is
